name: Logistics Shipment Analysis
description: Explores shipment performance across transport modes, routes, and carriers. Identifies delay patterns and cost drivers.

narration.pre: Starting a logistics performance analysis. We'll explore shipment data to understand on-time rates, route concentration, cost patterns, and delay outliers.
Importing shipment data: 10,000 shipments with origin/destination cities, transport mode (Air/Ground/Rail/Ocean), carrier, timestamps, weight, cost, and delay information.
narration.post: Dataset loaded: 10,000 shipments, 18 columns including freight_cost, delay_hours, on_time flag, priority level, and temperature-controlled indicator.
Insights ready. Let's explore the data.

details.sql: SELECT mode, COUNT(*) AS shipments, ROUND(AVG(total_cost), 2) AS avg_cost, ROUND(AVG(delay_hours), 1) AS avg_delay, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN on_time = 'Y' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS on_time_pct FROM dataset GROUP BY mode ORDER BY shipments DESC

narration.pre: Performance by transport mode: how do Air, Ground, Rail, and Ocean compare on volume, cost, delays, and on-time rate?
narration.post: 4 transport modes compared. Each mode has a different cost/speed tradeoff. Air is fastest but most expensive; Ocean is cheapest but slowest. On-time rates vary significantly by mode.

details.sql: SELECT origin_city, destination_city, COUNT(*) AS shipments, ROUND(AVG(total_cost), 2) AS avg_cost FROM dataset GROUP BY origin_city, destination_city ORDER BY shipments DESC LIMIT 10

narration.pre: Top 10 routes by shipment volume. Logistics networks tend to be heavily concentrated — a small number of city pairs handle most of the freight.
narration.post: Top 10 routes identified. These high-volume lanes are candidates for dedicated carrier contracts or rate negotiation. Average cost per route helps identify over- or under-priced lanes.

details.sql: SELECT on_time, COUNT(*) AS shipments, ROUND(AVG(total_cost), 2) AS avg_cost, ROUND(AVG(weight_kg), 1) AS avg_weight FROM dataset GROUP BY on_time ORDER BY on_time

narration.pre: On-time vs late: do late shipments cost more? Are they heavier? This tells us whether delays correlate with shipment characteristics or are purely operational.
narration.post: On-time vs late comparison. If late shipments are heavier or more expensive on average, that suggests capacity or routing issues on certain shipment types. If costs are similar, the delays are likely operational (carrier performance, weather, congestion).

details.sql: SELECT carrier, COUNT(*) AS shipments, ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN on_time = 'Y' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(*), 1) AS on_time_pct, ROUND(AVG(total_cost), 2) AS avg_cost FROM dataset GROUP BY carrier ORDER BY on_time_pct DESC LIMIT 10

narration.pre: Carrier scorecard: which carriers have the best on-time rates, and at what cost? This is the classic carrier performance analysis — balancing reliability against price.
narration.post: Top 10 carriers by on-time rate with their average cost. The best carriers aren't always the cheapest — the analysis reveals which carriers offer the best value (high reliability at reasonable cost).

details.sql: SELECT shipment_id, origin_city, destination_city, mode, carrier, delay_hours, total_cost FROM dataset WHERE delay_hours > 24 ORDER BY delay_hours DESC LIMIT 20

narration.pre: Delay outliers: shipments delayed more than 24 hours. These are the exceptions that warrant investigation — root cause analysis on the worst delays often reveals systemic issues.
narration.post: Top 20 most-delayed shipments. Each of these represents a service failure worth investigating. Patterns in the outliers (same carrier? same route? same mode?) point to the root cause.

Session summary: Analyzed 10,000 shipments across 4 transport modes. Compared on-time rates and costs by mode, identified top routes and best-performing carriers, and flagged delay outliers for investigation.

resume_state.last_sql: SELECT shipment_id, origin_city, destination_city, mode, carrier, delay_hours, total_cost FROM dataset WHERE delay_hours > 24 ORDER BY delay_hours DESC LIMIT 20
